mechanism_narrative: A falling federal funds rate lowers the discount rate on future cash flows and supports equity valuation. When FEDFUNDS is already near zero and continues to decline, the 20-day S&P 500 return should be positive.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with declining FEDFUNDS produce negative 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate signals weakening household income and earnings expectations. When UNRATE increases sharply, investors price lower near-term corporate profits, so S&P 500 returns over the next month should be negative.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3pp over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered unemployment spikes are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 55% of instances, or if the average triggered return is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in housing starts captures weakening residential demand and a broader cyclical growth slowdown. If HOUST falls materially from its recent level, S&P 500 returns over the next quarter should be negative as growth-sensitive sectors reprice lower.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls by more than 5% over 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if a 2-month HOUST decline above 5% is not followed by negative 60-day SPX returns on average, or if directional hit rate is below 50% over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Improving retail sales and industrial output together indicate broadening final demand and production momentum. When RSAFS and INDPRO both rise above their recent trend, the market should rally over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint positive-signal cases for RSAFS and INDPRO do not outperform single-series cases, or if triggered 20-day SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation is elevated while policy rates remain exceptionally low, real policy is very loose and the market interprets this as support for risk assets. A high CPIAUCSL with FEDFUNDS near zero should predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL is rising and FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5%
failure_condition: Reject if high-inflation/low-rate episodes do not produce positive 20-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the interaction term is not significant in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll growth reflects current labor income and aggregate spending power. A sustained decline in PAYEMS implies softer demand and earnings pressure, so S&P 500 returns over the next week should be negative.
failure_condition: Reject if negative PAYEMS momentum fails to precede negative 5-day SPX returns, or if the short-horizon Rank IC is indistinguishable from zero over any 18-month window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained fall in industrial production signals weakening current supply-side activity and softer realized corporate output. If INDPRO is below its recent trend, equities should underperform over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if below-trend INDPRO episodes are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if average forward returns are not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized slowdown in GDP, retail sales, and housing starts identifies a broad demand contraction that should hit equity earnings expectations. When GDPC1, RSAFS, and HOUST all weaken together, S&P 500 returns over the next month should be negative.
candidate_trigger: all three series are below their prior 3-month trend
failure_condition: Reject if synchronized weakness in GDP, retail sales, and housing does not reduce 20-day SPX returns versus baseline, or if the joint signal loses predictive power after controlling for any single proxy.
